response.justification: Sarah Smith is a capable polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript and Node.js credentials, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The job's core mandatory requirement is 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows; her only traceable C++ work amounts to approximately 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, totalling well under one year. She has no demonstrable exposure to fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking systems, and her database experience is centered on MongoDB and MySQL rather than the required SQL Server or Oracle. No optional skills such as Summit, Kafka, or Python are evidenced.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple engagements
Polyglot background with Java, DLang, and surface-level C/C++ indicating ability to learn new languages
Strong communication skills evidenced by conference talks, full-day workshops, and open-source community leadership
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows platforms
Zero financial domain knowledge — no fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking exposure anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is predominantly MongoDB and MySQL, mismatched to role requirements
